title: Centaurea olivieriana
taxonomy: Centaurea olivieriana is a species of thistle in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Centaurea and is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The species is further classified under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The plant is also classified as belonging to the class Magnoliopsida and the order Asterales. It is a part of the family of flowering plants known as Asteraceae.

intro: Centaurea olivieriana, also known as Olivier's knapweed, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea olivieriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from minor cuts and scrapes to more serious conditions such as arthritis and digestive disorders.
